MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Lynn R. Fiorentino, Esq., of NIXON PEABODY (415-984-8412), appearing for defendant / moving party Ford Motor Company;
Michael Levy, Esq., of BRENT COON & ASSOCIATES (415-489-7420), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Joseph Dazzo

RULING:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as revised: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability), 3 (failure to warn), and 7 (survival); and granted as to issues 4 (breach of implied warranty), 5 (fraud and conspiracy), 6 (false representation) and 8 (punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Dazzo was exposed to asbestos-containing brakes attributable to defendant. Diana Dazzo's deposition testimony and defendant's responses to plaintiffs' special interrogatories and requests for admission preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that any exposure to an asbestos-containing product for which defendant is liable was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Dazzo's mesothelioma. As to issue 4, plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable issue that decedent was in privity with Ford. As to issues 5 and 6, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant made any misrepresentation to Mr. Dazzo or committed any fraudulent concealment. As to issue 8, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Plaintiffs' objections to the deposition testimony of Dr. Hammar are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR #6840 = (220/HEK)
